title: 《自然》：喺對抗武漢肺炎方面，香港讓全世界上了一課

replies.msg:
Hong Kong seems to have given the world a lesson in how to effectively curb COVID-19. With a population of 7.5 million, it has reported just 4 deaths. Researchers studying Hong Kong’s approach have already found that swift surveillance, quarantine and social-distancing measures, such as the use of face masks and school closures, helped to cut coronavirus transmission — measured by the average number of people each infected person infects, or R — to close to the critical level of 1 by early February.

https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-020-01248-1

replies.msg:
直接去Nature 條link下面果格講

This is unrelated to "How do you see science changing after the pandemic?" but I feel unsatisfied with some points about Hong Kong made in the article.

I am a Hong Kong citizen who has experienced and witnessed how Hong Kong (both the government and ordinary citizens) handled Covid19 (or first known as Wuhan virus) for the whole journey.

When I read the article, I felt very uncomfortable with the unverified claim that
"Researchers studying Hong Kong’s approach have already found that swift surveillance, quarantine and social-distancing measures, such as the use of face masks and school closures, helped to cut coronavirus transmission"

The above conclusion is not reflecting the truth. In the very beginning of rumors of the virus and thereafter suppression of news by the China Central Government, Hong Kong government assured the public of “no need for masks and business as usual”. Health and travel declaration of commuters from outsides (mainly from China) were loosely implemented which led to the outbreak in Hong Kong. The so-called “swift surveillance” and “quarantine” were either ineffectively implemented or came only after the pressure from the public on the government. The credits for cutting the virus transmission in Hong Kong should be given only to:
1) Self-discipline and high awareness of Hong Kong people against the virus
2) Hong Kong people took no trust in the announcement of “no masks” and statements of undermining the virus severity made by China Central Government, Hong Kong government and WHO
3) Experience Hong Kong learnt from SARS in year 2003
4) Strike of the medical sector forcing the government to stop admission from outsiders (mainly from China) entering Hong Kong
5) Protests in year 2019 cut down a number of China Mainlanders travelling to Hong Kong
林超英

之前ig仲有鬼佬拍片講香港係反面教材
政府又贏

立場報道2020/1/31 — 19:46


武漢肺炎於本港確診數字不斷增加，全城「一罩難求」，更出現通宵排隊搶購口罩情況。但特首林鄭月娥今見記者時，未回應坊間要求向市民派發口罩，亦未有回應將防疫物資納入為儲備商品的訴求，她只是表示會優先保障醫護人員的口罩需要，更引用世衛指引稱，防疫不一定需要戴口罩。

林鄭月娥今於記者會上指，香港現時物資整體緊絀，但強調會優先保障醫護人員的物資需要。她預計未來市場的口罩供應會增加，但「唔夠膽講會增加到無短缺」，因全球醫護裝備使用量大增，相信供應緊張會持續一段時間，她又表示「世衞亦都出了指引，唔一定需要戴口罩，有咩情形下需要戴口罩，有咩情形之下，可以透過其他方法保護自己，同埋防止病毒感染，我們亦都會透過加強和公眾溝通，將這個信息傳播。」

林鄭又指，政府高度關注市民排隊搶購口罩情況，但強調由懲教處生產的口罩，只供政府使用，一般不應推出市場造成競爭，如有人偷賣懲教署口罩，政府將會嚴肅追究。

她又表示如果零售商有口罩出售，但數量不多，「零售商都要諗清楚，究竟用咩方法推廣呢個出售」，以免造成市民搶購的情況，期間她一度笑出聲。

商務及經濟發展局局長邱騰華則表示指，這段時間政府口罩的使用需求大增，特別是醫院的口罩需求，政府已經加大採購網絡，但由於主要供應來自內地，須安排清關等程序協助業界，以確保口罩及保護衣可如期或盡快運送到港。
所謂專家蠢撚到
關你政府閪事咩

根本香港就係靠唔信支共世衛同戴口罩

香港人戴罩係自己識保命
唔係正苦要求同強制人戴口罩
香港人善忘
所以係時候重溫

Lam said being as there is a shortage of masks in Hong Kong, officials should follow WHO advice that only certain groups of people need to wear them

信世衛，不怕
